name: cvt

members.name: CvTConfig
members.signature: CvTConfig(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, variant: str = 'cvt_13', dims: tuple[int, ...] = (64, 192, 384), depths: tuple[int, ...] = (1, 2, 10), num_heads: tuple[int, ...] = (1, 3, 6), embed_strides: tuple[int, ...] = (4, 2, 2), mlp_ratio: float = 4.0, dropout: float = 0.0, cls_token: tuple[bool, ...] = (False, False, True))

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, variant: str = 'cvt_13', dims: tuple[int, ...] = (64, 192, 384), depths: tuple[int, ...] = (1, 2, 10), num_heads: tuple[int, ...] = (1, 3, 6), embed_strides: tuple[int, ...] = (4, 2, 2), mlp_ratio: float = 4.0, dropout: float = 0.0, cls_token: tuple[bool, ...] = (False, False, True))

members.summary: Configuration dataclass for every CvT variant.
members.extended:
`CvTConfig` is an immutable container that fully specifies the
architecture of a Convolutional Vision Transformer (Wu et al.,
2021).  CvT replaces plain ViT's non-overlapping patch embedding
with *overlapping convolutional token embedding* at each of three
stages, and replaces the linear Q/K/V projections inside every
self-attention with *depthwise-separable convolutional projections*.

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description:
Number of output classes for the classification head.  Defaults
to `1000` (ImageNet-1k).

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of input image channels.  Defaults to `3` (RGB).

parameters.name: variant
parameters.description:
Informational label identifying the canonical variant
(`"cvt_13"`, `"cvt_21"`, `"cvt_w24"`).  Defaults to
`"cvt_13"`.

parameters.name: dims
parameters.description:
Per-stage hidden width (3 stages).  Defaults to
`(64, 192, 384)` (CvT-13).

parameters.name: depths
parameters.description:
Number of CvT blocks in each of the three stages.  Defaults
to `(1, 2, 10)` (CvT-13).

parameters.name: num_heads
parameters.description:
Number of attention heads per stage.  Defaults to `(1, 3, 6)`
(CvT-13).

parameters.name: embed_strides
parameters.description:
Stride of each stage's convolutional token embedding (the
spatial downsampling factor of the stage).  Defaults to
`(4, 2, 2)` — total downsampling 16x.

parameters.name: mlp_ratio
parameters.description:
Expansion ratio of the MLP inside each CvT block.  Defaults to
`4.0`.

parameters.name: dropout
parameters.description: Dropout probability inside MLP blocks.  Defaults to `0.0`.

examples:
Build a CvT-13 configuration with a 10-class head:
>>> from lucid.models.vision.cvt import CvTConfig
>>> cfg = CvTConfig(num_classes=10)
>>> cfg.dims, cfg.depths, cfg.num_heads, cfg.num_classes
((64, 192, 384), (1, 2, 10), (1, 3, 6), 10)

attributes.name: model_type
attributes.description: Constant string `"cvt"` used by the model registry.

members.name: CvT
members.extended:
The Convolutional vision Transformer (CvT) reinjects the locality
inductive bias of CNNs into ViT through two changes: (i) every
stage starts with an *overlapping convolutional token embedding*
(kernel > stride) that produces a new lower-resolution token grid,
and (ii) the Q / K / V projections inside every self-attention are
*depthwise-separable convolutions* rather than linear maps:

$$
Q = \mathrm{Flatten}(\mathrm{DWConv}_q(x_{2D})),\quad
K = \mathrm{Flatten}(\mathrm{DWConv}_k(x_{2D})),\quad
V = \mathrm{Flatten}(\mathrm{DWConv}_v(x_{2D})),
$$

where $x_{2D}$ is the spatially reshaped token map.  When the
K / V depthwise conv uses stride 2 the attention becomes *strided*,
reducing $N$ by 4x inside attention without losing the
full-resolution output for the next block.  CvT drops positional
embeddings entirely — locality is supplied implicitly by the
convolutional projections.

`forward_features` returns the mean-pooled token feature
$(B, \texttt{dims[-1]})$ over the final stage's tokens.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen dataclass specifying `dims`, `depths`, `num_heads`,
`embed_strides`, `mlp_ratio`, `dropout`, and
`in_channels`.  See `CvTConfig`.

examples:
Build a CvT-13 backbone and run a forward pass:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.cvt import CvT, CvTConfig
>>> model = CvT(CvTConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape                       # (B, dims[-1])
(1, 384)
notes:
Reference: Haiping Wu *et al.*, *"CvT: Introducing Convolutions to
Vision Transformers"*, ICCV 2021,
[arXiv:2103.15808](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.15808).

attributes.name: stages
attributes.description:
Three `_CvTStage` modules, each composed of a
convolutional token embedding plus a stack of CvT blocks.

attributes.name: feature_info
attributes.description:
Three-stage feature description with cumulative reductions
given by the cumulative product of `config.embed_strides`.

members.name: CvTForImageClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, labels: Tensor | None = None)

members.summary: CvT with a linear classification head (Wu et al., 2021).
members.extended:
Wraps the same three-stage trunk as `CvT` and adds a global
mean pool over tokens, a final LayerNorm, and a single
`nn.Linear` classification head:

$$
\text{logits} = W_{\text{head}}\,
    \mathrm{LN}\!\bigl(\mathrm{Mean}(z^{L})\bigr)
    + b_{\text{head}}.
$$

Pass `labels` to `forward` to compute the cross-entropy
loss in the same pass.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Architecture specification.  Must set `num_classes` to the
desired number of output categories.  See `CvTConfig`.

examples:
End-to-end inference with the default CvT-13 classifier:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.cvt import (
...     CvTConfig, CvTForImageClassification,
... )
>>> model = CvTForImageClassification(CvTConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Reference: Wu *et al.*, *"CvT: Introducing Convolutions to Vision
Transformers"*, ICCV 2021.  CvT-13 / CvT-21 reach **81.6% / 82.5%
top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 224x224 (Table 1 of the paper).

attributes.name: head_norm
attributes.description: Final LayerNorm applied to the mean-pooled feature.

attributes.name: classifier
attributes.description: Final linear projection of width `(num_classes, dims[-1])`.

members.name: cvt_13
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *CvT-13* configuration: `dims=(64, 192, 384)`,
`depths=(1, 2, 10)`, `num_heads=(1, 3, 6)`,
`embed_strides=(4, 2, 2)`.  Total spatial downsampling is
$4 \times 2 \times 2 = 16\times$, so a 224x224 input yields
a 14x14 final token grid.  Approximately **20M parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available in the model zoo.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides applied on top of the canonical CvT-13
config.  Each override must match a field of `CvTConfig`.

returns.description:
A `CvT` backbone returning a flat $(B, 384)$
mean-pooled feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.cvt import cvt_13
>>> model = cvt_13()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape
(1, 384)
notes:
CvT-13 reaches **81.6% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 224x224 (Wu et al.,
2021, Table 1).  See
[arXiv:2103.15808](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.15808).

members.name: cvt_13_cls
members.signature: cvt_13_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: CvT13Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `cvt_13` backbone with a mean pool + LayerNorm
+ single `nn.Linear` classification head.  Default output is
`num_classes=1000` (ImageNet-1k).  Approximately **20M parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CvT-13 config.

returns.description:
Classifier returning `ImageClassificationOutput` whose
`logits` has shape `(B, num_classes)`.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.cvt import cvt_13_cls
>>> model = cvt_13_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
CvT-13 reaches **81.6% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 224x224 (Wu et al.,
2021, Table 1).

members.name: cvt_21
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *CvT-21* configuration: same widths and head
counts as CvT-13 (`dims=(64, 192, 384)`, `num_heads=(1, 3, 6)`)
but deeper — `depths=(1, 4, 16)`.  Approximately **31.6M
parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CvT-21 config.

returns.description:
A `CvT` backbone returning a flat $(B, 384)$
mean-pooled feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.cvt import cvt_21
>>> model = cvt_21()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 384)
notes:
CvT-21 reaches **82.5% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 224x224 (Wu et al.,
2021, Table 1).

members.name: cvt_21_cls
members.signature: cvt_21_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: CvT21Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `cvt_21` backbone (`dims=(64, 192, 384)`,
`depths=(1, 4, 16)`) with a mean pool + LayerNorm + linear
classification head.  Approximately **31.6M parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CvT-21 config.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.cvt import cvt_21_cls
>>> model = cvt_21_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
CvT-21 reaches **82.5% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 224x224 (Wu et al.,
2021, Table 1).

members.name: cvt_w24
members.extended:
Builds the largest reference configuration *CvT-W24*:
`dims=(192, 768, 1024)`, `depths=(2, 2, 20)`,
`num_heads=(3, 12, 16)`.  Roughly an order of magnitude wider
than CvT-13.  Approximately **277.2M parameters** — typically used
only with large-scale (ImageNet-22k) pretraining.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-22k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CvT-W24 config.

returns.description:
A `CvT` backbone returning a flat $(B, 1024)$
mean-pooled feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.cvt import cvt_w24
>>> model = cvt_w24()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 1024)
notes:
CvT-W24 reaches **87.7% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 384x384 fine-tune
resolution after ImageNet-22k pretraining (Wu et al., 2021,
Table 4) — the headline result of the paper.

members.name: cvt_w24_cls
members.signature: cvt_w24_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: CvTW24Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: CvT-W24 wide image classifier (Wu et al., 2021).
members.extended:
Combines the `cvt_w24` backbone (`dims=(192, 768, 1024)`,
`depths=(2, 2, 20)`) with a mean pool + LayerNorm + linear
classification head.  Approximately **277.2M parameters** — the
largest CvT variant in the paper, typically used only with
ImageNet-22k pretraining.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-22k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CvT-W24 config.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.cvt import cvt_w24_cls
>>> model = cvt_w24_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
CvT-W24 reaches **87.7% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 384x384
fine-tune resolution after ImageNet-22k pretraining (Wu et al.,
2021, Table 4) — the headline result of the paper.

members.name: CvT13Weights
members.extended: Wu et al. ICCV 2021 13-layer convolutional ViT (20 M params, top-1 81.6%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import cvt_13_cls
>>> model = cvt_13_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Wu, Xiao, Codella, Liu, Dai, Yuan, Zhang, *"CvT:
Introducing Convolutions to Vision Transformers"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.15808](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.15808)).

members.name: CvT21Weights
members.extended: Wu et al. ICCV 2021 21-layer convolutional ViT (31.6 M params, top-1 82.5%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import cvt_21_cls
>>> model = cvt_21_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Wu, Xiao, Codella, Liu, Dai, Yuan, Zhang, *"CvT:
Introducing Convolutions to Vision Transformers"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.15808](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.15808)).

members.name: CvTW24Weights
members.extended: wide CvT-w24 IN-22k→IN-1k 384 finetune (Wu et al.; 277 M params, top-1 87.7%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import cvt_w24_cls
>>> model = cvt_w24_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Wu, Xiao, Codella, Liu, Dai, Yuan, Zhang, *"CvT:
Introducing Convolutions to Vision Transformers"*, ICCV 2021
([arXiv:2103.15808](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.15808)).

attributes.name: IN22K_FT_IN1K_384
attributes.description:
ImageNet-22k pretrain + ImageNet-1k 384x384 finetune (top-1
87.7%), sourced from `transformers/microsoft/cvt-w24-384-22k`.

summary: CvT family — backbone + image classification (Wu et al., 2021).
citation: Wu, Haiping, et al. "CvT: Introducing Convolutions to Vision Transformers." Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision, 2021, pp. 22-31.
theory:
The Convolutional vision Transformer (CvT) reinjects the locality
inductive bias of CNNs into ViT through two changes: (i) replacing
the non-overlapping patch embedding by *overlapping convolutional
token embedding*, and (ii) replacing the linear projections inside
self-attention with *depthwise-separable convolutional projections*.
CvT also drops positional embeddings entirely — locality is supplied
implicitly by the convolutional projections.

Convolutional token embedding is performed at the start of each
of the three stages: a strided convolution
$\mathrm{Conv}_{k \times k, \mathrm{stride}=s}$ produces a
new token grid of reduced spatial resolution and increased channel
width, yielding a Swin-like hierarchical pyramid.  Inside every
block the queries / keys / values are computed with depthwise
convolutions rather than linear maps:



$$
Q = \mathrm{Flatten}(\mathrm{DWConv}_q(x_{2D})),\quad
K = \mathrm{Flatten}(\mathrm{DWConv}_k(x_{2D})),\quad
V = \mathrm{Flatten}(\mathrm{DWConv}_v(x_{2D})),
$$

where $x_{2D}$ is the spatially reshaped token map.  When the
key/value DWConv uses stride 2 the attention becomes *strided*,
reducing $N$ by 4 inside attention without losing the
full-resolution output for the next block.  Variants CvT-13 / 21 /
24 scale depth and width along this three-stage convolutional
transformer skeleton.
